SEARCH
Package org.apache.arrow.datafusion.catalog

Interface SchemaProvider

All Known Implementing Classes:
NativeSchemaProvider
public interface SchemaProvider
A provider of tables within a catalog schema.

In DataFusion's catalog hierarchy, a SchemaProvider represents a database schema (namespace) that contains tables. Tables are referenced in SQL as catalog.schema.table.

Example implementation: 

public class MySchemaProvider implements SchemaProvider {
    private final Map<String, TableProvider> tables;

    @Override
    public List<String> tableNames() {
        return new ArrayList<>(tables.keySet());
    }

    @Override
    public Optional<TableProvider> table(String name) {
        return Optional.ofNullable(tables.get(name));
    }
}
See Also:
  • Rust DataFusion: SchemaProvider

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    default Optional<TableProvider>
    deregisterTable(String name)
    If supported by the implementation, removes the named table from this schema and returns the previously registered TableProvider, if any.
    default Optional<TableProvider>
    registerTable(String name, TableProvider table)
    If supported by the implementation, adds a new table to this schema.
    Optional<TableProvider>
    table(String name)
    Returns the table provider for the given table name.
    default boolean
    tableExists(String name)
    Checks if a table exists in this schema.
    List<String>
    tableNames()
    Returns the names of all tables in this schema.

  • Method Details

    • tableNames

      List<String> tableNames()
      Returns the names of all tables in this schema.

      Example: 

      @Override
public List<String> tableNames() {
    return new ArrayList<>(tables.keySet());
}
      Returns:
      List of table names
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SchemaProvider::table_names

    • table

      Optional<TableProvider> table(String name)
      Returns the table provider for the given table name.

      Example: 

      @Override
public Optional<TableProvider> table(String name) {
    return Optional.ofNullable(tables.get(name));
}
      Parameters:
      name - The table name
      Returns:
      The table provider, or null if the table doesn't exist
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SchemaProvider::table

    • registerTable

      default Optional<TableProvider> registerTable(String name, TableProvider table)
      If supported by the implementation, adds a new table to this schema.

      If a table of the same name was already registered, returns the previously registered table.

      Default implementation throws UnsupportedOperationException.

      Example: 

      @Override
public Optional<TableProvider> registerTable(String name, TableProvider table) {
    return Optional.ofNullable(tables.put(name, table));
}
      Parameters:
      name - The table name
      table - The table provider to register
      Returns:
      The previously registered table with the same name, if any
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if this schema does not support registering tables
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SchemaProvider::register_table

    • deregisterTable

      default Optional<TableProvider> deregisterTable(String name)
      If supported by the implementation, removes the named table from this schema and returns the previously registered TableProvider, if any.

      If no table with the given name exists, returns empty.

      Default implementation throws UnsupportedOperationException.

      Example: 

      @Override
public Optional<TableProvider> deregisterTable(String name) {
    return Optional.ofNullable(tables.remove(name));
}
      Parameters:
      name - The table name
      Returns:
      The previously registered table, if any
      Throws:
      UnsupportedOperationException - if this schema does not support deregistering tables
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SchemaProvider::deregister_table

    • tableExists

      default boolean tableExists(String name)
      Checks if a table exists in this schema.

      Default implementation checks if table(String) returns non-null.

      Example: 

      boolean exists = schemaProvider.tableExists("my_table");
      Parameters:
      name - The table name
      Returns:
      true if the table exists
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: SchemaProvider::table_exist
